2606.30304.title: Research Entity Extraction and Topic Detection from UKRI Grant Proposals
2606.30304.paper_summary: This paper presents preliminary findings from a UKRI-funded Metascience project comparing three LLM-based approaches, GPT-4o, Mistral, and a bespoke algorithm, DSIT-Taxonomies, for extracting and classifying research entities from funding proposals. Our project "Tracking Stars and Unicorns" aims to identify early signals of emerging research areas to inform public investment. Our methodology employed a three-stage pipeline, leveraging Mistral for primary entity extraction and mapping against the OpenAlex Topics taxonomy. We evaluated our approach across 42 proposals' abstracts from different areas and observed that Mistral and GPT-4o produce comparable, high-quality entity sets with significant semantic overlap, outperforming the fragmented DSIT-Taxonomies approach. Crucially, the Mistral-based approach achieved superior topic classification accuracy (90.5%) compared to the full DSIT-Taxonomies pipeline (71.4%). We conclude that Mistral offers a high-performance, operationally efficient, and secure solution for large-scale analysis of sensitive grant data.
2606.30304.paper_summary_zh: 這篇論文呈現了來自英國研究與創新局（UKRI）資助的元科學項目的初步發現，該項目比較了三種基於大型語言模型（LLM）的方法：GPT-4o、Mistral 和一個定制算法 DSIT-Taxonomies，用於從資助提案中提取和分類研究實體。<lb/>我們的項目「追蹤明星與獨角獸」旨在識別新興研究領域的早期信號，以指導公共投資。<lb/>我們的方法論採用了三階段的流程，利用 Mistral 進行主要實體的提取，並與 OpenAlex Topics 分類進行映射。<lb/>我們在來自不同領域的 42 份提案摘要中評估了我們的方法，觀察到 Mistral 和 GPT-4o 產生了可比較的高質量實體集，具有顯著的語義重疊，超越了分散的 DSIT-Taxonomies 方法。<lb/>關鍵是，基於 Mistral 的方法在主題分類準確性方面達到了優越的表現（90.5%），相比之下，完整的 DSIT-Taxonomies 流程僅為 71.4%。<lb/>我們得出結論，Mistral 提供了一個高性能、運營高效且安全的解決方案，適用於對敏感資助數據的大規模分析。
2606.30304.author: Xingran Ruan et.al.
2606.30304.authors: Xingran Ruan, Angelo Salatino, Rosa Filgueira, Kara Moraw, Alexandru Marcoci, Gemma Derrick, Sarah Callaghan
